title: echinops ritro subsp. ruthenicus
intro: Echinops ritro subsp. ruthenicus, also known as the Russian globe thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Echinops ritro subsp. ruthenicus is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in topical creams and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
